scope_constraint: Review delivered artifacts (template file, MCP runtime code edits, YAML wiring, skill docs, AGENTS triad, feature_catalog + playbook entries, end-to-end UX). DO NOT over-analyze the phase packet's own spec.md / plan.md / tasks.md / checklist.md / implementation-summary.md — those are scaffolding, not the deliverable.

01.title: Template design quality
01.questions: Is the shape correct? Are the 10 categories complete and non-overlapping? Is the Action/Status vocabulary clear? Are the author instructions actionable? Would a first-time user produce a useful catalog by copying and filling this template?

02.title: MCP runtime code correctness
02.focus: 9 TypeScript files edited in mcp_server/ (memory-types, memory-parser, graph-metadata-parser, resume-ladder, spec-doc-structure, memory-save, tool-schemas, 2 vitest files)
02.questions: Is the new filename consistently registered across all classifier sets, regexes, and priority lists? Are there remaining code paths that hardcode the old 9-doc list? Are the tier/weight defaults for resource_map appropriate? Does the classifier correctly distinguish resource-map.md from similar filenames?

03.title: Test coverage adequacy
03.focus: Existing vitest fixtures + any tests that should gain a resource-map.md case (per AUDIT-C: memory-types.vitest.ts:114-122 was flagged possible-miss; spec-doc-classification.vitest.ts; thin-continuity-record.vitest.ts)
03.questions: Does every test that enumerates canonical docs now include resource-map.md? Are there missing test cases for the new FILENAME_TO_DOC_TYPE entry? What about negative tests (files NOT matching resource-map regex)?

04.title: Command + YAML wiring consistency
04.questions: Do all 8 YAMLs include resource-map in consistent places (shared block, supporting-docs enumeration)? Is the YAML syntax valid? Do the two `save.md` edits align with each other and with the handler enumeration in memory-save.ts? Does resume.md agree with resume_auto.yaml?

05.title: Skill docs + references coherence
05.focus: SKILL.md, skill README.md, references/templates/*, references/structure/*, references/workflows/*, assets/*
05.questions: Does every table/tree/list that enumerates docs now include resource-map? Are there older references (pre-phase-012) still pointing at a 9-doc list that we missed? Does the ToC policy language include resource-map? Is wording consistent across files?

06.title: AGENTS triad sync + distributed governance rule
06.questions: Does the updated governance rule still make sense grammatically and semantically? Did we break the `research/iterations/*.md` exemption or the debug-delegation exclusive-access clause? Is the Barter sibling's slimmer phrasing still valid? Is the example-fs-enterprises documentation-levels note consistent?

07.title: End-to-end UX + feature-catalog/playbook fidelity
07.focus: feature_catalog/22.../25-resource-map-template.md + manual_testing_playbook/22.../270-resource-map-template.md + the operator journey (copy template -> fill -> memory:save)
07.questions: Do the feature catalog and playbook entries accurately describe the template and its wiring? Do they follow the neighbor-entry format (23-tool-routing-enforcement.md / 267-tool-routing-enforcement.md) closely? If an operator follows the updated docs end-to-end, does the workflow actually land cleanly (copy -> fill -> save -> index)? Where is the UX friction?
